title: Where AI Fits in Your Business
plainEnglishSummary: Lead gen, content, admin, and customer service — spot the opportunities in your own operation.

reading.script:
This week we're mapping AI onto your actual business — not theory, but where it fits in what you already do every week.

High-fit use cases I see over and over. Lead gen and follow-up — research, personalized openers, follow-up sequences, with a human hitting send. Content and marketing — posts, emails, landing copy from your briefs and voice notes. Admin and inbox — summarize threads, draft replies, turn meeting notes into action items. Customer service — FAQ drafts, tone rewrites, internal reply suggestions. Not unsupervised refunds or policy calls.

How do you spot opportunities in your own operation? List tasks you do weekly that involve words — reading, writing, sorting, summarizing. Ask yourself two questions: would a solid first draft save me twenty-plus minutes? And would a wrong draft hurt someone?

Bring your top two opportunities to the mastermind. That's high-value time. Alright — activity when you're ready.

sections.heading: High-fit use cases
paragraphs: Lead gen and follow-up — research, personalized openers, follow-up sequences. Human sends.
Content and marketing — posts, emails, landing copy from your briefs and voice notes.
Admin and inbox — summarize threads, draft replies, meeting notes into action items.
Customer service — FAQ drafts, tone rewrites, internal reply suggestions. Not unsupervised refunds or policy calls.

sections.heading: Spot opportunities in your business
paragraphs: List tasks you do weekly that involve words: reading, writing, sorting, summarizing.
Ask: would a solid first draft save me twenty-plus minutes? Would a wrong draft hurt someone?
Bring your top two opportunities to the mastermind — that's high-value session time.

discussPrompts: What's your #1 AI opportunity this month?
What should you NOT delegate to AI in customer service?

steps.situation: New leads get a generic template 2 days late.
steps.question: Best AI assist?
options: Draft personalized openers from CRM notes — you review and send same day
Fully autonomous send with no human
steps.feedback: Speed + personalization draft = lead gen win. Human approves.

steps.situation: You record voice notes after sales calls and need LinkedIn posts.
steps.question: Best fit?
Smart first step?
options: AI summarizes each thread + drafts replies for you to approve
steps.feedback: Admin/inbox = summarize + draft. You stay in control.

steps.situation: Customer asks if you can honor a policy exception on a refund.
steps.question: AI's role?
options: Draft options for you — you decide and respond
steps.feedback: CS policy = human decision. AI can prep language only.

steps.situation: You're spotting AI opportunities in your business.
steps.question: Best filter?
options: Weekly word-heavy tasks where a draft saves 20+ min and errors are fixable
Only tasks you've never done before
Only tasks that require 100% accuracy with no review
steps.feedback: Repeatable language work + review = sweet spot.

steps.situation: You're preparing for mastermind with Kai.
steps.question: What to bring?
options: Top 2 workflows, time spent, and where you're stuck
A request to 'do AI for everything'
steps.feedback: Specific workflows = specific help. That's ROI on the session.

takeaway: Lead gen, content, admin, CS drafts — not high-stakes autopilot. Bring workflows to mastermind.
